translation: With the approval of the Brāhmaṇas, he went through the expiatory rites prescribed for moving out of the sacrificial precincts during the consecration period.

translation:
Lord Kṛṣṇa said:
“I shall have to come personally. It won’t be possible (for you to protect the child), O Arjuna. The spirit that killed the child, appears to be extra-ordinary.”

devanagari: स आत्मन्युत्थितम्मन्युमात्मजायात्मना प्रभुः । अशीशमद्यथा वह्निं स्वयोन्या वारिणात्मभूः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ātmanyutthitammanyumātmajāyātmanā prabhuḥ aśīśamadyathā vahniṃ svayonyā vāriṇātmabhūḥ
translation: Having performed the concluding bath (avabhṛtha majjana) in the great river Yamunā, the excellent member of the Yadu race passed his time happily in the company of great sages.

devanagari: ततः कैलासमगमत्स तं देवो महेश्वरः । परिरब्धुं समारेभे उत्थाय भ्रातरं मुदा ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kailāsamagamatsa taṃ devo maheśvaraḥ parirabdhuṃ samārebhe utthāya bhrātaraṃ mudā
translation: The demons Nivāta-Kavacas, the residents of Hiraṇyapura, who could not be conquered even by Indra, were killed by me in a fight.

devanagari: नैच्छत्त्वमस्युत्पथग इति देवश्चुकोप ह । शूलमुद्यम्य तं हन्तुमारेभे तिग्मलोचनः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: naicchattvamasyutpathaga iti devaścukopa ha śūlamudyamya taṃ hantumārebhe tigmalocanaḥ

translation: Can there be any evil spirit more powerful than that, O slayer of demons? Why do you frighten me? Please give me permission to go just now.”

Kṛṣṇa who was thus persuaded, gave permission to Arjuna to go. Arjuna, equipped with his (Gāṇḍīva) bow, went out of the sacrificial hall.

devanagari: शयानं श्रिय उत्सङ्गे पदा वक्षस्यताडयत् । तत उत्थाय भगवान्सह लक्ष्म्या सतां गतिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śayānaṃ śriya utsaṅge padā vakṣasyatāḍayat tata utthāya bhagavānsaha lakṣmyā satāṃ gatiḥ

translation: He ascended his spacious chariot with the banner of the monkey emblem hoisted over it. He placed the Brāhmaṇa in front of him and rode round the village.

devanagari: स्वतल्पादवरुह्याथ ननाम शिरसा मुनिम् । आह ते स्वागतं ब्रह्मन्निषीदात्रासने क्षणम् । अजानतामागतान्वः क्षन्तुमर्हथ नः प्रभो ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: svatalpādavaruhyātha nanāma śirasā munim āha te svāgataṃ brahmanniṣīdātrāsane kṣaṇam ajānatāmāgatānvaḥ kṣantumarhatha naḥ prabho

translation: Pāṇḍavas and other kings who were eager to see the sacrifice and were invited by Kṛṣṇa came to Dvārakā, O King Parīkṣit.

devanagari: पुनीहि सहलोकं मां लोकपालांश्च मद्गतान् । पादोदकेन भवतस्तीर्थानां तीर्थकारिणा ॥ १० ॥

translation: While the glorious celebration of the horse-sacrifice was going on, Brāhmaṇas got thoroughly satisfied with the feasts and gifts of cloth, cows, land and gold.

devanagari: अद्याहं भगवंल्लक्ष्म्या आसमेकान्तभाजनम् । वत्स्यत्युरसि मे भूतिर्भवत्पादहतांहसः ॥ ११ ॥

translation: Being initiated as a sacrificer, Śrī Kṛṣṇa saw to it that the prominent Brāhmaṇas got to their satisfaction all their desired objects as well as fed with whatever they wished to eat.

translation:
Worshipful sire! From to-day I have been the sole abode of goddess Lakṣmī. With my sins completely washed off by the touch of your feet (which symbolise knowledge) the goddess of prosperity (Lakṣmī in the form of Śrī Vatsa mark) shall ever reside on my bosom.”
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text:]
12(A). And let the mark (of your foot-print on my bosom) be known by the name Śrī Vatsa from to-day.”

devanagari: पुनश्च सत्रमाव्रज्य मुनीनां ब्रह्मवादिनाम् । स्वानुभूतमशेषेण राजन्भृगुरवर्णयत् ॥ १३ ॥

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When Lord Viṣṇu addressed him thus, Bhṛgu felt deeply satisfied and happy with his melodious words in a deep low tone. He remained silent as his voice was choked with over-whelming feeling of devotion, his eyes, over-flowing with tears.

devanagari: तन्निशम्याथ मुनयो विस्मिता मुक्तसंशयाः । भूयांसं श्रद्दधुर्विष्णुं यतः शान्तिर्यतोऽभयम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tanniśamyātha munayo vismitā muktasaṃśayāḥ bhūyāṃsaṃ śraddadhurviṣṇuṃ yataḥ śāntiryato

translation: For god Viṣṇu is directly the fountain head of righteousness, spiritual knowledge, renunciation (of four kinds) in association with eight kinds of mystical powers (Siddhis) and glory, which cleanly washes off the dirt (sins) of the soul.

devanagari: सत्त्वं यस्य प्रिया मूर्तिर्ब्राह्मणास्त्विष्टदेवताः । भजन्त्यनाशिषः शान्ता यं वा निपुणबुद्धयः ॥ १७ ॥

translation: His beloved personality is constituted of pure Sattva- guṇa; Brāhmaṇas, his beloved deities. Persons of keen and subtle intellect who are of serene mind and who covet for no blessings, resort to him.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं सारस्वता विप्रा नृणाम्संशयनुत्तये । पुरुषस्य पदाम्भोज सेवया तद्गतिं गताः ॥ १९ ॥

translation: He has three forms which are invested of Māyā consisting of three guṇaṣ: The Rākṣasa, the Tāmasic form, Asura, the Rājasic and gods, the Sāttvic form. (He is unattached to all these guṇas but) Sattva is the means of attaining the lore of Bhakti and he is thus associated with Sattva.

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, the Brāhmaṇas who were performing the sacrifice, came to this conclusion for dispelling the doubts of the people. By worshipping the lotus-feet of the Supreme Person, they attained to his blissful state.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एकदा द्वारवत्यां तु विप्रपत्न्याः कुमारकः । जातमात्रो भुवं स्पृष्ट्वा ममार किल भारत ॥ २१ ॥

translation:
Sūta said:
This story is the very nectar scented with the fragrance of the Lotus-mouth of Śuka, the son of the sage Vyāsa. It celebrates the glory of the Supreme Person of hallowing renown. It dispels the fear of the cycle of births and deaths (saṃsāra). The wanderer in this cycle of transmigration, who constantly drinks this nectar with the goblets of his ears, gives up fatigue of wandering through the paths of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: विप्रो गृहीत्वा मृतकं राजद्वार्युपधाय सः । इदं प्रोवाच विलपन्नातुरो दीनमानसः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vipro gṛhītvā mṛtakaṃ rājadvāryupadhāya saḥ idaṃ provāca vilapannāturo dīnamānasaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka began to narrate:
It is traditionally reported that on one occasion, at Dvārakā, the newly born son of a Brāhmaṇa woman died immediately as he touched the ground (i.e. the moment she delivered the child, it expired), O descendant of Bharata!

devanagari: ब्रह्मद्विषः शठधियो लुब्धस्य विषयात्मनः । क्षत्रबन्धोः कर्मदोषात्पञ्चत्वं मे गतोऽर्भकः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmadviṣaḥ śaṭhadhiyo lubdhasya viṣayātmanaḥ kṣatrabandhoḥ karmadoṣātpañcatvaṃ me gato

translation:
Being initiated as a sacrificer, Śrī Kṛṣṇa saw to it that the prominent Brāhmaṇas got to their satisfaction all their desired objects as well as fed with whatever they wished to eat.
23-13. While kings occupied their respective seats all round and the cane-bearers, making a sound of the canes, were declaring “Silence please,” a certain Brāhmaṇa with roots of grass (or a cane) in his hand, rushed in and standing at the gate of the sacrificial hall, raised-up his hands and cried out loudly in affliction. His throat was parched with hunger, face dried up tears were flowing from his eyes and with distressed heart, he cried out thus:

devanagari: हिंसाविहारं नृपतिं दुःशीलमजितेन्द्रियम् । प्रजा भजन्त्यः सीदन्ति दरिद्रा नित्यदुःखिताः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: hiṃsāvihāraṃ nṛpatiṃ duḥśīlamajitendriyam prajā bhajantyaḥ sīdanti daridrā nityaduḥkhitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sad class 1 verb]

translation: “I have lost my child, because of the sin of a base kṣattriya (ruler) who is a hater of Brāhmaṇas, wicked-minded, greedy and voluptuous.

devanagari: एवं द्वितीयं विप्रर्षिस्तृतीयं त्वेवमेव च । विसृज्य स नृपद्वारि तां गाथां समगायत ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ dvitīyaṃ viprarṣistṛtīyaṃ tvevameva ca visṛjya sa nṛpadvāri tāṃ gāthāṃ samagāyata
translation: When (sympathetically) enquired thus, the Brāhmaṇa reported: “As soon as my child is born it is immediately taken away that very moment from the lying-in-chamber by some unknown agency, dear Sir.”

devanagari: तामर्जुन उपश्रुत्य कर्हिचित्केशवान्तिके । परेते नवमे बाले ब्राह्मणं समभाषत ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmarjuna upaśrutya karhicitkeśavāntike parete navame bāle brāhmaṇaṃ samabhāṣata
translation: In this manner, the Brāhmaṇa sage screamed out the same condemning appeal the second and the third time and left the dead body of the child at the royal gate.

devanagari: किं स्विद्ब्रह्मंस्त्वन्निवासे इह नास्ति धनुर्धरः । राजन्यबन्धुरेते वै ब्राह्मणाः सत्रमासते ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ svidbrahmaṃstvannivāse iha nāsti dhanurdharaḥ rājanyabandhurete vai brāhmaṇāḥ satramāsate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Hearing that cry while he was near Śrī Kṛṣṇa Arjuna enquired with the Brāhmaṇa when his nineth child was lost.

devanagari: धनदारात्मजापृक्ता यत्र शोचन्ति ब्राह्मणाः । ते वै राजन्यवेषेण नटा जीवन्त्यसुम्भराः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanadārātmajāpṛktā yatra śocanti brāhmaṇāḥ te vai rājanyaveṣeṇa naṭā jīvantyasumbharāḥ

translation: “Is there no Kṣattriya in your house (i.e. in this city) who can wield a bow? These Kṣattriyas (Yādavas who cannot protect their subjects) are not better than Brāhmaṇas engaged in this sacrifice.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
These Brāhmaṇas are performing this sacrifice for these wretched Kṣattriyas who cannot protect their subjects—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: अहं प्रजाः वां भगवन्रक्षिष्ये दीनयोरिह । अनिस्तीर्णप्रतिज्ञोऽग्निं प्रवेक्ष्ये हतकल्मषः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ prajāḥ vāṃ bhagavanrakṣiṣye dīnayoriha anistīrṇapratijño

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ īkṣ class 1 verb]

translation: The Kṣattriya (rulers) of that Kingdom where Brāhmaṇas being robbed of their wealth and being bereaved of their wives and children, have to grieve helplessly, are verily no better than actors on the stage who fill their belly by playing the role of Kṣattriyas.

devanagari: श्रीब्राह्मण उवाच । सङ्कर्षणो वासुदेवः प्रद्युम्नो धन्विनां वरः । अनिरुद्धोऽप्रतिरथो न त्रातुं शक्नुवन्ति यत् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrāhmaṇa uvāca saṅkarṣaṇo vāsudevaḥ pradyumno dhanvināṃ varaḥ aniruddho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: O worshipful Sir, as both of you are so helpless here, I hereby undertake to protect the child that will be born of you. If I fail to fulfil my pledge, I shall enter fire (and burn myself down to death) and expiate myself of that sin.”

devanagari: तत्कथं नु भवान्कर्म दुष्करं जगदीश्वरैः । त्वं चिकीर्षसि बालिश्यात्तन्न श्रद्दध्महे वयम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatkathaṃ nu bhavānkarma duṣkaraṃ jagadīśvaraiḥ tvaṃ cikīrṣasi bāliśyāttanna śraddadhmahe vayam
translation:
The Brāhmaṇa remarked:
Saṅkarṣaṇa (Balarāma), Śrī Kṛṣṇa, Pradyumna the foremost archer and Aniruddha, an unparalleled hero have not been able to protect my child.

devanagari: श्रीअर्जुन उवाच । नाहं सङ्कर्षणो ब्रह्मन्न कृष्णः कार्ष्णिरेव च । अहं वा अर्जुनो नाम गाण्डीवं यस्य वै धनुः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīarjuna uvāca nāhaṃ saṅkarṣaṇo brahmanna kṛṣṇaḥ kārṣṇireva ca ahaṃ vā arjuno nāma gāṇḍīvaṃ yasya vai dhanuḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Even the Lords of the world found the achievement of this feat impossible. It is childish on your part to boast like this. We simply don’t believe in it.

devanagari: मावमंस्था मम ब्रह्मन्वीर्यं त्र्यम्बकतोषणम् । मृत्युं विजित्य प्रधने आनेष्ये ते प्रजाः प्रभो ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: māvamaṃsthā mama brahmanvīryaṃ tryambakatoṣaṇam mṛtyuṃ vijitya pradhane āneṣye te prajāḥ prabho

translation:
Arjuna said:
O Brāhmaṇa, I am neither Balarāma, nor Kṛṣṇa nor Kṛṣṇa’s descendant. I am verily that Arjuna, the wielder of the bow famous by the name Gāṇḍīva.

devanagari: एवं विश्रम्भितो विप्रः फाल्गुनेन परन्तप । जगाम स्वगृहं प्रीतः पार्थवीर्यं निशामयन् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ viśrambhito vipraḥ phālgunena parantapa jagāma svagṛhaṃ prītaḥ pārthavīryaṃ niśāmayan

translation:
Arjuna said:
10 “Go to your house, O Brāhmaṇa. I have given complete protection to you on all sides. Please go and comfort your wife who must be afraid.”

devanagari: प्रसूतिकाल आसन्ने भार्याया द्विजसत्तमः । पाहि पाहि प्रजां मृत्योरित्याहार्जुनमातुरः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: prasūtikāla āsanne bhāryāyā dvijasattamaḥ pāhi pāhi prajāṃ mṛtyorityāhārjunamāturaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ bhṛ ], [genitive single from √ bhṛ ]

translation: The Brāhmaṇa, so confidently assured by Arjuna, went home pleased at heart, as he had heard the exploits of Arjuna.

devanagari: स उपस्पृश्य शुच्यम्भो नमस्कृत्य महेश्वरम् । दिव्यान्यस्त्राणि संस्मृत्य सज्यं गाण्डीवमाददे ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa upaspṛśya śucyambho namaskṛtya maheśvaram divyānyastrāṇi saṃsmṛtya sajyaṃ gāṇḍīvamādade

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sañj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sañj class 1 verb]

translation: The Brāhmaṇa also entered that lying-in-chamber and in a short while cried for Arjuna’s help.

devanagari: न्यरुणत्सूतिकागारं शरैर्नानास्त्रयोजितैः । तिर्यगूर्ध्वमधः पार्थश्चकार शरपञ्जरम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: nyaruṇatsūtikāgāraṃ śarairnānāstrayojitaiḥ tiryagūrdhvamadhaḥ pārthaścakāra śarapañjaram

translation: Arjuna sipped water (as Ācamana) and bowing to the great god Śiva, he invoked all his divine missiles and kept his bow Gāṇḍīva ready.

devanagari: ततः कुमारः सञ्जातो विप्रपत्न्या रुदन्मुहुः । सद्योऽदर्शनमापेदे सशरीरो विहायसा ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kumāraḥ sañjāto viprapatnyā rudanmuhuḥ sadyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single], [aorist middle first single]

translation: Arjuna as it were encaged the lying-in-chamber by creating a fence of arrows charged with mantras of various missiles on all side, above and below that chamber.

devanagari: तदाह विप्रो विजयं विनिन्दन्कृष्णसन्निधौ । मौढ्यं पश्यत मे योऽहं श्रद्दधे क्लीबकत्थनम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadāha vipro vijayaṃ vinindankṛṣṇasannidhau mauḍhyaṃ paśyata me yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ nind class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ nind class 1 verb]

translation: Then the wife of that Brāhmaṇa gave birth to a son who cried well (repeatedly), but in an instant, he bodily vanished through the sky.

devanagari: न प्रद्युम्नो नानिरुद्धो न रामो न च केशवः । यस्य शेकुः परित्रातुं कोऽन्यस्तदवितेश्वरः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: na pradyumno nāniruddho na rāmo na ca keśavaḥ yasya śekuḥ paritrātuṃ ko
translation: Then the Brāhmaṇa censured Arjuna in the presence of Kṛṣṇa blaming himself, “Look at my folly, in placing faith in the empty bragging of an impotent person.

devanagari: धिगर्जुनं मृषावादं धिगात्मश्लाघिनो धनुः । दैवोपसृष्टं यो मौढ्यादानिनीषति दुर्मतिः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: dhigarjunaṃ mṛṣāvādaṃ dhigātmaślāghino dhanuḥ daivopasṛṣṭaṃ yo mauḍhyādāninīṣati durmatiḥ
translation: Who else can be capable enough to protect my child whom neither Pradyumna, nor Aniruddha, nor Balarāma nor Śrī Kṛṣṇa were able to protect.

devanagari: एवं शपति विप्रर्षौ विद्यामास्थाय फाल्गुनः । ययौ संयमनीमाशु यत्रास्ते भगवान्यमः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ śapati viprarṣau vidyāmāsthāya phālgunaḥ yayau saṃyamanīmāśu yatrāste bhagavānyamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Fie upon Arjuna, the boastful liar! Fie upon the bow of his who indulges in self-advertisement (self-flattery). The wicked fellow, out of his stupidity, brags to restore back what has been snatched away by Fate.”

devanagari: विप्रापत्यमचक्षाणस्तत ऐन्द्री मगात्पुरीम् । आग्नेयीं नैरृतीं सौम्यां वायव्यां वारुणीमथ ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: viprāpatyamacakṣāṇastata aindrī magātpurīm āgneyīṃ nairṛtīṃ saumyāṃ vāyavyāṃ vāruṇīmatha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: While the Brāhmaṇa sage was reproaching him, Arjuna used his mystical lore and quickly went to Saṃyamanī, the capital of the god of death (Yama).

devanagari: रसातलं नाकपृष्ठं धिष्ण्यान्यन्यान्युदायुधः । ततोऽलब्धद्विजसुतो ह्यनिस्तीर्णप्रतिश्रुतः । अग्निं विविक्षुः कृष्णेन प्रत्युक्तः प्रतिषेध...
english_devnagari: rasātalaṃ nākapṛṣṭhaṃ dhiṣṇyānyanyānyudāyudhaḥ tato agniṃ vivikṣuḥ kṛṣṇena pratyuktaḥ pratiṣedhatā
translation: Not being able to trace the child of that Brāhmaṇa he visited (and explored) the capital cities of the great gods like Indra, Agni, Nairṛta, Soma, Vāyu and Varuṇa and with his weapon ready, he went to the subterranean regions and to regions higher than the celestial region and many other regions.

devanagari: दर्शये द्विजसूनूंस्ते मावज्ञात्मानमात्मना । ये ते नः कीर्तिं विमलां मनुष्याः स्थापयिष्यन्ति ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: darśaye dvijasūnūṃste māvajñātmānamātmanā ye te naḥ kīrtiṃ vimalāṃ manuṣyāḥ sthāpayiṣyanti

translation: As he could not trace the child and had failed to fulfill his pledge, he prepared himself to enter fire but was prohibited by Kṛṣṇa from doing so.

devanagari: इति सम्भाष्य भगवानर्जुनेन सहेश्वरः । दिव्यं स्वरथमास्थाय प्रतीचीं दिशमाविशत् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sambhāṣya bhagavānarjunena saheśvaraḥ divyaṃ svarathamāsthāya pratīcīṃ diśamāviśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: (Kṛṣṇa said) “I shall show you the sons of that Brāhmaṇa. Please do not disrespect yourself in this way. Those (who are censuring you now) will permanently establish (and sing of) our pure spotless glory.

devanagari: सप्त द्वीपान्ससिन्धूंश्च सप्त सप्त गिरीनथ । लोकालोकं तथातीत्य विवेश सुमहत्तमः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: sapta dvīpānsasindhūṃśca sapta sapta girīnatha lokālokaṃ tathātītya viveśa sumahattamaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having comforted him with these words, the glorious Almighty Lord ascended his divine chariot along with Arjuna and sped towards the west.

devanagari: तत्राश्वाः शैब्यसुग्रीव मेघपुष्पबलाहकाः । तमसि भ्रष्टगतयो बभूवुर्भरतर्षभ ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāśvāḥ śaibyasugrīva meghapuṣpabalāhakāḥ tamasi bhraṣṭagatayo babhūvurbharatarṣabha
translation: Then passing over the seven island-continents and the seven oceans dividing those continents, the seven mountain-ranges and the Lokāloka mountain[2] (which divides the visible world from the region of darkness), he entered the region of darkness.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
A mythical mountain that encircles the earth and is beyond the seven continents and the encircling sea of fresh water. The sun’s rays do not reach beyond that mountain.

devanagari: तान्दृष्ट्वा भगवान्कृष्णो महायोगेश्वरेश्वरः । सहस्रादित्यसङ्काशं स्वचक्रं प्राहिणोत्पुरः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tāndṛṣṭvā bhagavānkṛṣṇo mahāyogeśvareśvaraḥ sahasrādityasaṅkāśaṃ svacakraṃ prāhiṇotpuraḥ
translation: O prominent Bharata! In that pitch-black darkness, his horses, Śaibya, Sugrīva, Meghapuṣpa and Balāhaka lost their way and could not proceed.

devanagari: तमः सुघोरं गहनं कृतं महद् । विदारयद्भूरितरेण रोचिषा । मनोजवं निर्विविशे सुदर्शनं । गुणच्युतो रामशरो यथा चमूः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: tamaḥ sughoraṃ gahanaṃ kṛtaṃ mahad vidārayadbhūritareṇa rociṣā manojavaṃ nirviviśe sudarśanaṃ guṇacyuto rāmaśaro yathā camūḥ

translation: Noticing their plight, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Master of all experts in Yoga, discharged ahead his discus Sudarśana which was effulgent like one thousand suns shining simultaneously.

devanagari: द्वारेण चक्रानुपथेन तत्तमः परं परं ज्योतिरनन्तपारम् । समश्नुवानं प्रसमीक्ष्य फाल्गुनः प्रताडिताक्षो पिदधेऽक्षिणी उभे ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: dvāreṇa cakrānupathena tattamaḥ paraṃ paraṃ jyotiranantapāram samaśnuvānaṃ prasamīkṣya phālgunaḥ pratāḍitākṣo pidadhe
translation: Piercing through that extremely horrendous, palpably thick all-pervading darkness, the effect of Prakṛti or created by the Lord himself with its exceedingly enormous lustre, the Sudarśana discus with the speed of the mind (quick like a thought or imagination) cut its way like the arrow of Rāma discharged from the bow-string penetrated through hostile army.

devanagari: ततः प्रविष्टः सलिलं नभस्वता बलीयसैजद्बृहदूर्मिभूषणम् । तत्राद्भुतं वै भवनं द्युमत्तमं भ्राजन्मणिस्तम्भसहस्रशोभितम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ praviṣṭaḥ salilaṃ nabhasvatā balīyasaijadbṛhadūrmibhūṣaṇam tatrādbhutaṃ vai bhavanaṃ dyumattamaṃ bhrājanmaṇistambhasahasraśobhitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[nominative single from √ bṛh ], [vocative single from √ bṛh ], [accusative single from √ bṛh ]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Following the path cut by the discus, (the chariot reached the end of darkness), Arjuna saw that Supreme, Infinite, dazzling lustre beyond that dark-region (of Prakṛti). But his eyes became dazed and he closed the eyes.

devanagari: तस्मिन्महाभोगमनन्तमद्भुतं । सहस्रमूर्धन्यफणामणिद्युभिः । विभ्राजमानं द्विगुणेक्षणोल्बणं । सिताचलाभं शितिकण्ठजिह्वम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminmahābhogamanantamadbhutaṃ sahasramūrdhanyaphaṇāmaṇidyubhiḥ vibhrājamānaṃ dviguṇekṣaṇolbaṇaṃ sitācalābhaṃ śitikaṇṭhajihvam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ si class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ si class 9 verb]
[vocative single from √ si class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ si class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ si class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ si class 9 verb]

translation: Thereafter he entered water (the ocean) adorned with huge billows lashed up by powerful tempestuous gales. Verily he saw then a wonderful mansion highly resplendent with thousands of columns of brilliant jewels.

devanagari: ददर्श तद्भोगसुखासनं विभुं । महानुभावं पुरुषोत्तमोत्तमम् । सान्द्रा म्बुदाभं सुपिशङ्गवाससं । प्रसन्नवक्त्रं रुचिरायतेक्षणम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: dadarśa tadbhogasukhāsanaṃ vibhuṃ mahānubhāvaṃ puruṣottamottamam sāndrā mbudābhaṃ supiśaṅgavāsasaṃ prasannavaktraṃ rucirāyatekṣaṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: And in that mansion, he beheld the presence of the Serpent Śeṣa (Ananta) who was the most formidable yet wonderful to look at. He was radiant with brilliant jewels on each of his thousand hoods, with two fierce eyes. His body was white like the Himālayas but was black at the neck and in the tongue.

devanagari: महामणिव्रातकिरीटकुण्डल । प्रभापरिक्षिप्तसहस्रकुन्तलम् । प्रलम्बचार्वष्टभुजं सकौस्तुभं । श्रीवत्सलक्ष्मं वनमालयावृतम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: mahāmaṇivrātakirīṭakuṇḍala prabhāparikṣiptasahasrakuntalam pralambacārvaṣṭabhujaṃ sakaustubhaṃ śrīvatsalakṣmaṃ vanamālayāvṛtam
translation: He beheld the all-pervading Lord of infinite powers resting on the soft and comfortable coils of serpent Śeṣa. The complexion of that most exalted Super-human being was dark like a cloud surcharged with water. He wore a yellow garment. He had a cheerful countenance and beautiful longish eyes.

devanagari: महामणिव्रातकिरीटकुण्डल । प्रभापरिक्षिप्तसहस्रकुन्तलम् । प्रलम्बचार्वष्टभुजं सकौस्तुभं । श्रीवत्सलक्ष्मं वनमालयावृतम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: mahāmaṇivrātakirīṭakuṇḍala prabhāparikṣiptasahasrakuntalam pralambacārvaṣṭabhujaṃ sakaustubhaṃ śrīvatsalakṣmaṃ vanamālayāvṛtam
translation: The effulgence of the numerous precious jewels in his crown and the refulgence of his ear-rings enveloped ṭlike a halo) and lit up his profuse curly locks of hair. He had eight Jong beautiful arms. He wore the brilliant Kaustubha gem round his neck and had the Śrī Vatsa mark (on his chest), and he was decorated with Vanamālā (a garland of five coloured forest flowers reaching upto the knees).

devanagari: ववन्द आत्मानमनन्तमच्युतो जिष्णुश्च तद्दर्शनजातसाध्वसः । तावाह भूमा परमेष्ठिनां प्रभुर्बद्धाञ्जली सस्मितमूर्जया गिरा ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: vavanda ātmānamanantamacyuto jiṣṇuśca taddarśanajātasādhvasaḥ tāvāha bhūmā parameṣṭhināṃ prabhurbaddhāñjalī sasmitamūrjayā girā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He was the Lord of Supreme gods who was attended upon by Sunanda and Nanda, the chief of his guards, by the discus Sudarśana and other weapons in a corporal form and by his four potencies, viz. Puṣṭi (grace), Śrī (splendour, beauty and affluence), Kīrti (Fame) and the Invincible potency (Māyā) and eight miraculous powers (Siddhis).

devanagari: द्विजात्मजा मे युवयोर्दिदृक्षुणा मयोपनीता भुवि धर्मगुप्तये । कलावतीर्णाववनेर्भरासुरान्हत्वेह भूयस्त्वरयेतमन्ति मे ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: dvijātmajā me yuvayordidṛkṣuṇā mayopanītā bhuvi dharmaguptaye kalāvatīrṇāvavanerbharāsurānhatveha bhūyastvarayetamanti me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ rā class 2 verb]

translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa bowed to the Infinite Lord who was himself in another form, Arjuna filled with awe at his sight, paid him obeisance. Both of them stood with folded palms. The Perfect Person, the controller of Brahmā and other lords (presiding deities) of the spheres, smilingly addressed in a deep and sonorous voice to them.

devanagari: पूर्णकामावपि युवां नरनारायणावृषी । धर्ममाचरतां स्थित्यै ऋषभौ लोकसङ्ग्रहम् ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrṇakāmāvapi yuvāṃ naranārāyaṇāvṛṣī dharmamācaratāṃ sthityai ṛṣabhau lokasaṅgraham
translation: “The sons of the Brāhmaṇa were brought by me desirous of seeing you both. Both of you have incarnated on the earth by your part-manifestations for the protection of righteousness. Having killed the demons who have become a burden to the earth as soon as possible, return to our presence.

devanagari: इत्यादिष्टौ भगवता तौ कृष्णौ परमेष्ठिना । ॐ इत्यानम्य भूमानमादाय द्विजदारकान् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: ityādiṣṭau bhagavatā tau kṛṣṇau parameṣṭhinā oṃ ityānamya bhūmānamādāya dvijadārakān
translation: Both of you are famous sages Nara and Nārāyaṇa who have already accomplished all blessings. But for the maintenance of the world-order, you both have set a model of righteousness for others to follow.

devanagari: न्यवर्तेतां स्वकं धाम सम्प्रहृष्टौ यथागतम् । विप्राय ददतुः पुत्रान्यथारूपं यथावयः ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: nyavartetāṃ svakaṃ dhāma samprahṛṣṭau yathāgatam viprāya dadatuḥ putrānyathārūpaṃ yathāvayaḥ
translation: Thus commanded by the glorious Supreme Deity, those two dark-complexioned heroes (Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) accepted the mandate, respectfully bowed to the Perfect Being, and taking with them the sons of the Brāhmaṇa returned with delighted hearts to their home by the same route they came. They restored to the Brāhmaṇa his sons grown up according to their age but similar in appearance as their father saw them, (at the time of their birth).

devanagari: निशाम्य वैष्णवं धाम पार्थः परमविस्मितः । यत्किञ्चित्पौरुषं पुंसां मेने कृष्णानुकम्पितम् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: niśāmya vaiṣṇavaṃ dhāma pārthaḥ paramavismitaḥ yatkiñcitpauruṣaṃ puṃsāṃ mene kṛṣṇānukampitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Thus commanded by the glorious Supreme Deity, those two dark-complexioned heroes (Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna) accepted the mandate, respectfully bowed to the Perfect Being, and taking with them the sons of the Brāhmaṇa returned with delighted hearts to their home by the same route they came. They restored to the Brāhmaṇa his sons grown up according to their age but similar in appearance as their father saw them, (at the time of their birth).

devanagari: इतीदृशान्यनेकानि वीर्याणीह प्रदर्शयन् । बुभुजे विषयान्ग्राम्यानीजे चात्युर्जितैर्मखैः ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: itīdṛśānyanekāni vīryāṇīha pradarśayan bubhuje viṣayāngrāmyānīje cātyurjitairmakhaiḥ

translation:
Having performed the concluding bath (avabhṛtha majjana) in the great river Yamunā, the excellent member of the Yadu race passed his time happily in the company of great sages.
[Here ends the hundredth chapter in Padaratnāvalī’s text]

devanagari: प्रववर्षाखिलान्कामान्प्रजासु ब्राह्मणादिषु । यथाकालं यथैवेन्द्रो भगवान्श्रैष्ठ्यमास्थितः ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: pravavarṣākhilānkāmānprajāsu brāhmaṇādiṣu yathākālaṃ yathaivendro bhagavānśraiṣṭhyamāsthitaḥ
translation: Displaying in this way many feats and exploits, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, enjoyed worldly pleasures as well as worshipped the Supreme Deity by performing great sacrifices on an exceedingly superior scale.

devanagari: हत्वा नृपानधर्मिष्ठान्घाटयित्वार्जुनादिभिः । अञ्जसा वर्तयामास धर्मं धर्मसुतादिभिः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: hatvā nṛpānadharmiṣṭhānghāṭayitvārjunādibhiḥ añjasā vartayāmāsa dharmaṃ dharmasutādibhiḥ
translation: The glorious Lord was himself endowed with overflowing affluence, power, etc. just as god Indra showers down water profusely in the proper (i.e. the rainy) season, he also in due time incessantly bestowed all the desired-objects on his subjects, Brāhmaṇas and others.
